title: aira capillaris subsp. tenorei
intro: Aira capillaris subsp. tenorei, also known as Tenore's hair grass, is a subspecies of the Aira capillaris plant, a type of grass native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Aira capillaris subsp. tenorei include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exerting antioxidant effects, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Aira capillaris subsp. tenorei can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the dried leaves and stems in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds in a solvent.
